content: Prostate cancer is the most common cancer in men in developed countries. PSA (prostate-specific antigen) screening is controversial due to overdiagnosis of indolent cancers. Risk stratification guides management: very low/low risk (PSA <10, Gleason 6, T1-T2a, <50% cores positive); favorable intermediate (one intermediate factor); unfavorable intermediate (multiple intermediate factors or Gleason 4 pattern predominant); high (PSA >20, Gleason 8-9, T3); very high (T4, Gleason 10, primary Gleason 5). Gleason scoring (Grade Groups 1-5): Grade Group 1 (Gleason 3+3=6), 2 (3+4=7), 3 (4+3=7), 4 (8), 5 (9-10); Grade Group predicts prognosis. Genomic classifiers (Decipher, Oncotype DX Prostate, Prolaris) refine risk assessment. Molecular features: TMPRSS2-ERG fusion (~50%), PTEN loss, TP53 mutations in advanced disease; HRR gene mutations (BRCA2, ATM, others) in 10-20% metastatic; MSI-H rare (<3%).
